module_purpose: Convert the user query into a finalized visualization intent with map-first requirements, time-awareness, and downstream execution guidance for data preparation, plotting, and insight generation.
user_query: Show residential sales density in Baner and Balewadi from 2021 to 2024.in 3d heatmap timelapse
business_objective: Visualize how residential sales density changed across Baner and Balewadi over time from 2021 to 2024 using a time-aware map-based output.

additional_parameters.time_requirement_reason: A multi-period time range or trend was detected, so the map must be time-aware with a time-slider/timelapse payload.
additional_parameters.timelapse_rule: Keep the primary map type unchanged and add a time-slider/timelapse payload for multi-period time ranges.

execution_plan.step_purpose: Confirm the business objective, metric, geography, filters, time requirement, map output requirement, and downstream workflow route.
validation_checks: business_objective is present or inferable
structured_intent is available
map_output_requirements.is_active is true
at least one selected_map_type is present
required_modules and execution_flags are consistent with active requirement blocks
execution_plan.skip_condition: Never skip. This is the first mandatory workflow step.
execution_plan.failure_handling: If minimum intent cannot be inferred, set clarification_required=true and stop before data processing.

execution_plan.step_name: Receive retrieved data from Data Retrieval Agent
execution_plan.step_purpose: Accept the dataset already retrieved by the Data Retrieval Agent and prepare it for compatibility checks.
validation_checks: retrieved_dataset is available
dataset has rows and columns
dataset is readable as tabular or structured records
column_metadata is available or can be inferred
execution_plan.skip_condition: Never skip when the user expects a data-backed visualization.
execution_plan.failure_handling: Return data_missing status if the retrieved dataset is empty or unavailable.

execution_plan.step_name: Map retrieved columns to intent requirements
execution_plan.step_purpose: Map source columns to the fields required by the finalized intent.
validation_checks: metric/source measure field is mapped or derivable
location/geography field is mapped or derivable
time field is mapped when time_field_required is true
filter fields are mapped when filters are present
map tooltip candidate fields are identified where possible
execution_plan.skip_condition: Skip only if the retrieved dataset already follows the standard visualization schema.
execution_plan.failure_handling: Use intent_mapping fallback candidates; if still unresolved, mark missing_field_requirements for the conversational clarification layer.

execution_plan.step_name: Filter dataset based on finalized intent
execution_plan.step_purpose: Apply user-intent filters such as location, property segment, project, date range, price range, or category.
validation_checks: all filter fields exist or have valid fallback mappings
time filters are applied when time_field_required is true
filtered_dataset is not empty
excluded records are counted or logged for transparency
execution_plan.skip_condition: Skip only if the user query contains no filters and full retrieved data should be used.
execution_plan.failure_handling: If filtering returns no records, return filter_no_result with filter summary and candidate fallback values.

execution_plan.step_name: Aggregate and restructure data for visualization
execution_plan.step_purpose: Convert filtered data into an analysis-ready dataset at the required metric, geography, dimension, and time granularity.
validation_checks: aggregation level matches geo_level
base_map_metric is computed or derivable
time buckets are created when time_field_required is true
timelapse frame key is created when timelapse_required is true
analysis_ready_dataset contains usable records for map plotting
execution_plan.skip_condition: Skip aggregation only if the selected map type requires raw row-level plotting.
execution_plan.failure_handling: If metric aggregation fails, return aggregation_failed and include missing numeric/date fields.

execution_plan.step_purpose: Apply scenario assumptions and create a simulated dataset when simulation_requirements is active.
validation_checks: simulation_requirements.is_active is false
execution_plan.failure_handling: If unsupported, return simulation_skipped_or_unsupported and continue with base analysis_ready_dataset.

execution_plan.step_purpose: Compare the base dataset with a changed or simulated scenario when what_if_requirements is active.
validation_checks: what_if_requirements.is_active is false
execution_plan.failure_handling: If comparison cannot be computed, return what_if_skipped_or_unsupported and continue with available outputs.

execution_plan.step_name: Validate map output requirements against prepared data
execution_plan.step_purpose: Confirm that the selected map type, metric, geo level, time/timelapse requirement, and layer requirements can be produced from the prepared data.
validation_checks: primary_map_type is supported
base_map_metric exists in analysis or scenario output
geo_level is available or mappable
time field exists when time_field_required is true
timelapse frame field exists when timelapse_required is true
layer_requirements are internally consistent with selected_map_types
execution_plan.skip_condition: Never skip because map output is mandatory for this Visualization Agent.
execution_plan.failure_handling: Fallback to the simplest valid map type, usually marker_map or 2d_heatmap.

execution_plan.step_purpose: Convert analysis/scenario output into map-compatible records with coordinates, geometry, metric values, tooltip fields, and optional time frames.
validation_checks: latitude/longitude or boundary/centroid is available or mappable
each map feature has a metric value
tooltip fields are available or generated from mapped fields
time frame field is preserved for time-aware maps
records are grouped at the intended geo_level
execution_plan.skip_condition: Skip only if analysis_ready_dataset is already in the finalized frontend map contract.
execution_plan.failure_handling: Use static location lookup or centroid fallback.

execution_plan.step_purpose: Generate the actual first-iteration plotted map output/configuration.
validation_checks: selected map types are supported: ['3d_heatmap']
primary map type is generated: 3d_heatmap
map layer data follows frontend contract
tooltip metadata is attached
time-slider/timelapse payload is attached
execution_plan.skip_condition: Never skip because a visual/map output is required for every user query.
execution_plan.failure_handling: Fallback to marker_map with metric annotations if the selected map output cannot be generated.

execution_plan.step_purpose: Analyze geographic patterns from the plotted map output when spatial_requirements is active.
validation_checks: spatial_requirements.is_active is false
execution_plan.failure_handling: If advanced context layers are unavailable, generate basic spatial interpretation from plotted map output.

execution_plan.step_purpose: Generate the final user-facing response by combining the intent, prepared data, plotted map output, and any simulation/what-if/spatial outputs that were executed.
validation_checks: final response is aligned with business_objective
insights are supported by computed outputs
map output is included or referenced
skipped modules are not treated as executed outputs
limitations/fallbacks are mentioned when relevant
execution_plan.skip_condition: Never skip because final user-facing response is required.
execution_plan.failure_handling: Generate a limited response from available successful outputs and clearly state missing/failed workflow parts.

intent_mapping.mapping_purpose: Define how the query intent should be translated into downstream field usage for filtering, aggregation, geo-plotting, and insight generation without ambiguity.

target_geographies.meaning: List of requested locations to include in the visualization.
target_geographies.downstream_use: Module 2 filters records to these geographies; Module 3 maps them to spatial units; Module 7 compares them in the summary.

time_range.meaning: Start and end years defining the time window for the density analysis.
time_range.downstream_use: Module 2 filters by date/year; Module 3 groups records by year; Module 7 summarizes year-wise trends.

time_granularity.meaning: Temporal bucket size used for aggregation and timelapse frame generation.
time_granularity.downstream_use: Module 2 and Module 3 use this to aggregate values annually and create frame sequence labels.

timelapse_required.meaning: Indicates the visualization must animate across time.
timelapse_required.downstream_use: Module 3 must output frame-ready data and enable time-slider plotting.

needs_timelapse_layer.meaning: Signals that the selected map type must include a layered time dimension.
needs_timelapse_layer.downstream_use: Module 3 should preserve per-period outputs rather than collapsing all years into a single static layer.

preferred_density_view.meaning: Preferred rendering style for density visualization.
preferred_density_view.downstream_use: Module 3 uses this to choose 3D surface-style density shaping rather than marker-only output.

aggregation_dimension.meaning: The primary bucket used when computing density values.
aggregation_dimension.downstream_use: Module 2 groups by year and geography; Module 3 renders each aggregated bucket as a frame or layer.

visual_encoding.meaning: Rules for encoding data dimensions into visual channels.
visual_encoding.downstream_use: Module 3 maps sales density to height and color, and year to frame progression.

selected_map_types.meaning: Map visualization types selected for the query.
selected_map_types.source: User-specified visualization or backend fallback selection.

primary_map_type.meaning: Primary map visualization that should drive the first plotted output.
primary_map_type.source: User visualization intent or inferred fallback logic.

timelapse_requirements.meaning: Time-aware map requirement used to build a time-slider or timelapse payload without changing the primary map type.
timelapse_requirements.source: Derived from user-provided time range or temporal comparison intent.

Data Restructuring & Filtering: Use target_geographies to filter the dataset
Use time_range and time_granularity to filter and aggregate records
Use primary_subject to select the correct sales-density measure
Geo-Enrichment & Map Plotting: Use selected_map_types and primary_map_type to choose rendering path
Use geo_level to determine spatial resolution
Use timelapse_required and needs_timelapse_layer to enable frame-based plotting
Use visual_encoding to assign visual channels
Simulation Depiction Layer: Only activate if a future scenario or changed_case appears in the intent
Use base_map_metric and additional_parameters for overlay construction
What-if Analysis Engine: Only activate if a changed case is defined versus a base case
Use comparison_mode-like parameters if added later in extensions
Spatial Analysis: Use plotted_map_output as the mandatory spatial input dependency
Only perform if new spatial analysis flags are introduced
Insight Generation: Use the plotted map output as the primary source
Use time_range and geographies to summarize trends and differences
Use yearly aggregation outputs to identify peak and low periods

fallback_mapping.if_density_not_available: Use transaction count as a proxy density metric and label it clearly as count-based density proxy.
fallback_mapping.if_exact_boundary_missing: Use centroid or micromarket boundary approximation for Baner and Balewadi.
fallback_mapping.if_year_field_missing: Attempt to derive year from transaction date; if unavailable, mark timelapse as degraded and preserve static map output if possible.
fallback_mapping.if_3d_rendering_fails: Fallback to 2d_heatmap or 2d_overlay while retaining time slider support.
fallback_mapping.if_timelapse_not_supported: Return a static year-filtered map for the latest available year and flag degraded temporal fidelity.

notes_for_downstream_modules: Do not reinterpret the geography list as a single combined area; preserve both Baner and Balewadi as distinct comparison targets.
The query is explicitly time-bounded, so yearly buckets are preferred unless the source data only supports a coarser or finer consistent grain.
A 3D heatmap timelapse should remain the primary target; downgrade only if technical constraints force it.
Insight generation should emphasize both spatial difference and temporal evolution rather than only one dimension.
